programmes.show_description: The Stockbroker's Clerk. Enjoy this classic short story read by Mike Read where a young clerk consults Holmes with his suspicions concerning a company that has offered him a very well paid job.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life The Throwaway Society
programmes.show_description: 1968. A fascinating look at the different types of rubbish generated by society, and the problems of how to dispose of it, including recycling.
The Getaway - Part 1. 1974. Stars John Barron, Jonathan Elsom & John Flanagan. Phil McDowell is on trial for the abduction of Fiona Sumner, a fourteen year old girl. He denies the offense.
The Getaway - Part 2. 1974. Stars Lyndon Brook, Elizabeth Weaver & Tina Heath. Fiona Sumner tells the court she initially agreed to go with McDowell but later changed her mind.
The Getaway - Part 3. 1974 Stars John Flanagan, John Barron & Lyndon Brook. Son of Philip McDowell tells the court that Fiona wanted to go on the trip to visit her father who she thought was dead.
1958. War. Director: Vernon Sewell. Stars Michael Rennie, Patricia Medina & Christopher Lee. Polish Resistance discover a mysterious pilot-less plane.
1963. Who Killed the Kind Doctor? Directed by Don Taylor. Stars Gene Barry, Gary Conway & Regis Toomey. An LA psychiatrist telephones Burke to warn him of an impending murder by one of his patients.
1960. The Show-Off. Stars Michael Ansara, Scott Marlowe & Joan Lora. A young man rides into town and allows everyone to believe he is Billy the Kid. Sam isn't sure if his claim is true or not.
1961. Crime. Directed by Montgomery Tully. Stars Gordon Jackson, Christina Gregg & Lisa Daniely. Tom's wedding day becomes a confusing nightmare when a stranger appears, claiming to be his wife.
1955. Comedy. Director: Vernon Sewell. A cockney family inherit a ramshackle farm. Alfie (Leslie Dwyer) wants to make a go of it. Starring George Cole and Kathleen Harrison.
1950. Drama. A lowly bank clerk marries a good time girl but she two-times him for her old crooked boyfriend (Sydney Tafler). Starring Jack Watling, Pat Kirkwood and Thora Hird.
Comedy. Director: David Paltenghi. Stars Arthur Askey, Glenn Melvyn & Thora Hird. Two football loving railway workers get into trouble after racing their engine home to get to a match on time.
Backfire. 1961. Directed by Paul Almond. Stars Alfred Burke, Zena Marshall, Oliver Johnston & Noel Treverthen. Everything points to prosperity in this luxurious factory! Or so it seems...
1963. Western. Director: Andrew V. McLaglen. Stars John Wayne, Maureen O'Hara & Patrick Wayne. Wealthy rancher McLintock uses his power and influence to keep the peace between conflicting groups.
The Fine Print. 1960. Stars John Ireland & Nora Nicholson. A woman is unable to claim the money from her late father's insurance policy after he forgot to read the fine print. Can anyone help her?
1946. Drama. Directed by Robert Hamer. Stars Googie Withers, Mervyn Johns & Gordon Jackson. Victorian Brighton; the tale of a pious chemists' household & the underworld occupied by landlady Pearl.
1959. Drama. A burglar murders an old woman but a blind girl 'witnesses' the robber as he flees. Stars Patricia Dainton, Conrad Phillips, Nigel Green & Richard O'Sullivan.
On Watch - Newtown. 1962. Stars Joseph Brady, Brian Blessed & Jeremy Kemp. Barlow is searching for counterfeit money, meanwhile Steele and Lynch are tracking Ralph before he kills his father.
Caroline Munro is back to open the Cellar Club for another night of horror! Kicking off the show tonight is the fourth episode of Supernatural starring Jeremy Brett: Mr Nightingale.
Mr. Nightingale. 1977. Stars Jeremy Brett, Lesley-Anne Down & Susan Maudslay. An Englishman travels to Hamburg for business but finds a mysterious double is haunting him.
Up next, Caroline Munro prepares you for the 1960s horror film Devil's Corner.
1960. Horror. Director: Charles R. Rondeau. Stars Edgar Buchanan, Jean Allison & Richard Crane. An old man sells his soul to the devil to become young and win a woman from his rival.
Caroline Munro closes tonight's show and covers what to expect next week at the Cellar Club.

programmes.show_name: Hammer House of Mystery and Suspense
programmes.show_description: The Late Nancy Irving. 1984. Stars Cristina Raines & Marius Goring. A victim of a car accident finds herself in hospital and discovers that she is not a patient but a prisoner...
1960. Horror. Directed by Ernest Morris. Stars Laurence Payne, Adrienne Corri & Dermot Walsh. A man is tortured by the sound of his friend's beating heart after killing him in a jealous rage.

programmes.show_name: The Man with the Golden Arm
programmes.show_description: 1955. Drama. Directed by Otto Preminger. Stars Frank Sinatra, Kim Novak & Eleanor Parker. The story of a recovering drug addict who must battle to stay clean after being released from prison.
1965. A Neat Little Package. Classic crime series produced by Aaron Spelling and starring Anne Francis. A newsdealer survives a car wreck with amnesia and a mysterious $175,000.
1935. Western. Director: Harry S. Webb. Stars Jack Perrin, Fred Humes, Philo McCullough. Cactus Jack is blamed for murder after his partner is found with a knife in his back.
Made in 1934 screen songs animated short. Produced by Fleischer Studios. Director Dave Fleischer. The song featured in this cartoon was 'I'm Popeye The Sailor Man written by Sammy Lerner.
Trapped. 1974. Stars John Sanderson, Roberta Tovey & Mark Donaldson. Peterkin remains trapped inside Lady Ann's cottage. Meanwhile, Lord William barges in on Lady Ann's wedding.
1997. Stars Brendan Pollecutt, Fezile Mpela & Robert Finlayson. Dean and Thabo are in serious trouble until the young princess of the hidden city helps them.
The Black Castle. 1956. Stars William Russell, Cyril Smith & Ronald Leigh-Hunt. Torwald holds Lord Trebizond to ransom in the Black Castle. Lorraine, rides to Camelot to seek help.
Ice Spy. 1967. Stars Adam West, Burt Ward & Alan Napier. Mr. Freeze is back in town and he plans to build an icy weapon that will have the world at his control.
Hidden Treasure. 1954. Stars Roy Rogers, Trigger & Dale Evans. After overhearing Dale tell of where great treasure can be found, the outlaws will stop at nothing to get their hands on it.
Winged Assassin. 1967. Stars Ed Bishop, Jeremy Wilkin & Francis Matthews. Spectrum Organisation attempts to stop the Mysterons from assassinating the Director General of the United Asian Republic.
Boy Slaves. 1958. Stars: Conrad Phillips, Willoughby Goddard & Jennifer Jayne. Can Tell trust the word of his enemy? He must, if he's to obtain the release of young boys whom Gessler is holding.
The Articles of War. 1956. Stars Robert Shaw, Eric Pohlmann & Brian Rawlinson. The island is running out of food and a Spanish naval ship threatens to destroy the next shipment.
Tables Turned. 1956. Stars Richard Greene, Victor Woolf & Archie Duncan. Messengers are guarding the jewels, but loose talking allows Derwent to her about it; so, he plans to steal them!
1965. Sci-fi. Directed by Koreyoshi Akasaka, Teruo Ishii & Akira Mitsuwa. Stars Ken Utsui & Sachihiro Ohsawa. Starman is sent to Earth after learning of a devastating plot to destroy the planet.
1947. Crime Drama. Directed by John Paddy Carstairs. Stars Richard Attenborough, Barry K. Barnes, Sheila Sim & Garry Marsh. A Cab driver gets mistaken with a gang. He sets out to expose the group.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life Gold in the Mud
programmes.show_description: 1969. An interesting look at the world of scampi which has increased in popularity.
Escape. 1958. Stars Robert Beatty, Jack Lambert & Harry Towmb. Following a tip-off, Bill Killian is caught opening a safe. His brother helps him escape but the police are hot on their trail.
The Twisted Road. 1961. Stars Peter Graves, Anthony Wickert & Tom Farley. Cobb must deliver a prisoner safely to Brisbane in order for him to face charges after being accused of murder.
1958. War. Guy Green. Stars Richard Attenborough, Michael Craig & John Gregson. The Long Range Desert Group go on an expedition through North Africa to destroy a huge Nazi fuel dump.
A late 50s local authority travelogue showcasing the wonders of the great Scottish city of Aberdeen.
Seven Little Crosses. 1962. Stars Rupert Davies, Alfred Burke & James Maxwell. The young son of a murder suspect leads the police on a clever trail. Is he purposely hindering or helping the case?

programmes.show_name: Sherlock Holmes: Hound of the Baskervilles
programmes.show_description: 1988. Drama. Director: Brian Mills. Stars Jeremy Brett, Edward Hardwicke & Raymond Adamson. Can Sherlock find the truth when the heir to the Baskerville estate is threatened?
1953. Thriller. Director: Hugo Fregonese. Stars Jack Palance, Constance Smith & Byron Palmer. A landlady suspects her new tenant is Jack the Ripper when a self-described pathologist moves in.
Short documentary film detailing Hollywood's fascination with the heavenly, the devilish and the supernatural, including the Topper film series and It's A Wonderful Life
